ddls:
CREATE TABLE "control_parameters" (
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each control parameter */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the control parameter */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the parameter and its usage */,
  "min_value" REAL /* Minimum allowable value for the parameter */,
  "max_value" REAL /* Maximum allowable value for the parameter */,
  "default_value" REAL /* Default value assigned to the parameter */,
  "units" TEXT /* Units of measurement for the parameter */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the drone model this parameter is associated with */,
  "operational_mode" TEXT /* Operational mode the parameter is associated with (e.g., acro, assist, auto) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the parameter was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the parameter */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the user who created the parameter */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the user who last updated the parameter */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("parameter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "drone_models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each drone model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the drone model */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the drone model */,
  "max_speed" REAL /* Maximum speed the drone can achieve */,
  "max_altitude" REAL /* Maximum altitude the drone can reach */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "parameter_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each change in the parameter */,
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the control parameter that was changed */,
  "old_value" REAL /* The value of the parameter before the change */,
  "new_value" REAL /* The value of the parameter after the change */,
  "changed_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the change was made */,
  "changed_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the user who made the change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, technician, admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the control parameter that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* The message to be displayed in the alert */,
  "alert_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the alert was triggered */,
  "alert_status" TEXT /* Status of the alert (e.g., active, resolved) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id")
)
